---
title: "Shapley values"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Shapley values}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

# Regression example

```{r fig.width=7}
library(unifiedml) # this package
library(randomForest)

# Create data with or without column names
set.seed(42)
X <- matrix(rnorm(100), ncol=4)
# No column names initially - function will add X1, X2, etc.
y <- 2*X[,1] - 1.5*X[,2] + rnorm(25)

# Fit model
mod <- Model$new(randomForest::randomForest)
mod$fit(X, y, ntree=100)

# Compute SHAP values (automatically handles missing column names)
(shap_result <- unifiedml::compute_shap_values(mod))
# Message: "Added column names: X1, X2, X3, X4"

# View feature importance
colMeans(abs(shap_result$S))

# With meaningful column names
colnames(X) <- c("Age", "Income", "Education", "Experience")
mod2 <- Model$new(randomForest::randomForest)
mod2$fit(X, y, ntree=100)
(shap_result2 <- unifiedml::compute_shap_values(mod2))  # Uses your meaningful names
```


```{r fig.width=7}
#library(shapviz)

# Full analysis with importance plot
analysis <- unifiedml::analyze_shap(mod, plot_type = "importance")

# Access results
analysis$feature_importance
analysis$plots$importance

# Summary plot (both bar and beeswarm)
(analysis2 <- unifiedml::analyze_shap(mod, plot_type = "summary"))

# Dependence plot for specific feature
(analysis3 <- unifiedml::analyze_shap(mod, plot_type = "dependence", feature = "X1"))
```


# Compare multiple models 

```{r}
# Train different models
mod_rf <- Model$new(randomForest::randomForest)
mod_rf$fit(X, y, ntree=100)

mod_lm <- Model$new(lm)  
mod_lm$fit(X, y)

# Compare SHAP importance
comparison <- unifiedml::compare_shap_importance(
  models = list(mod_rf, mod_lm),
  model_names = c("Random Forest", "Linear Regression"),
  X = X
)
print(comparison)
```
